focus: Native-Speed Shadowing & Transcription — Blueprint Phase 3/4 Methodology
focus_ar: المحاكاة والتفريغ بالسرعة الطبيعية — منهجية المرحلة ٣/٤ من الخطة
arabic_challenge: The blueprint's 5-Phase Shadowing Protocol (§5.5) assigns Phase 3 (Full-Speed shadowing while reading) to L1-L2, and Phase 4 (Transcription, then shadowing from your own transcription) specifically to L2. This week integrates that transcription methodology directly into accent training: you cannot accurately transcribe native-speed audio without first correctly perceiving the reductions, linking, and merged sounds you've spent 10 weeks learning to PRODUCE — this week trains you to also RECOGNIZE them by ear.

target_sounds: transcription practice: identifying reduced forms by ear

isolation.instructions: Listen to (or have a buddy/AI read) a short native-speed sentence containing several reduced forms, and try to transcribe EXACTLY what you hear, including the reductions — not the 'correct/formal' spelling.
isolation.instructions_ar: استمع (أو خد صاحب/AI يقرا) جملة بسرعة طبيعية فيها صيغ مخففة، وحاول تفرغ بالضبط اللي سمعته، بما فيها التخفيضات — مش الإملاء الرسمي.
practice: Listen to: 'I shoulda called her.' → Transcribe what you actually hear (shoulda, not 'should have')
Listen to: 'Whaddya want?' → Transcribe the reduced form you hear

pair: transcribing the ACTUAL reduced sound heard

sentence_practice: Gimme a minute, and lemme think about it.
daily_drills.record_this: I shoulda called her yesterday — whaddya want for dinner? Gimme a minute, and lemme think about it.
daily_drills.self_check: Could you both PRODUCE these casual reduced forms AND accurately identify them by ear from someone else's speech?

target_sounds: transcription practice: linking and flap in fast speech

isolation.instructions: Practice transcribing a fast sentence containing L1's linking and flap patterns, marking exactly where words blend together as you hear them.
isolation.instructions_ar: تدرب على تفريغ جملة سريعة فيها أنماط الربط والفلاب من المستوى الأول، وحدد بالضبط فين الكلمات بتندمج زي ما بتسمعها.
practice: Listen to: 'Pick up an apple and gimme some water.' → Mark every linking point and flap you hear.

sentence_practice: Pick up an apple and gimme some water.
Press the button on the machine, would ya?
I need a little water and an apple, thanks.
daily_drills.record_this: Pick up an apple and gimme some water — press the button on the machine, would ya?
daily_drills.self_check: When listening back to a recording of this sentence, could you identify every linking and flap point accurately?

target_sounds: shadowing from your own transcription (Blueprint Phase 4)

isolation.instructions: Take your transcription from Day 1 or 2, and now SHADOW it — read it aloud simultaneously with the original audio, matching the reduced pronunciation exactly as you transcribed it.
isolation.instructions_ar: خد التفريغ بتاعك من يوم ١ أو ٢، ودلوقتي حاكيه — اقراه بصوت عالي في نفس وقت الصوت الأصلي، مطابقًا النطق المخفف بالضبط زي ما فرغته.
practice: Shadow: 'I shoulda called her yesterday' — matching rhythm, reduction, and speed exactly.

pair: shadowing that matches the original's reduction and speed
shadowing that reverts to full, careful pronunciation

sentence_practice: Pick up an apple and gimme some water.
daily_drills.record_this: (Shadow your own Day 1/2 transcriptions, matching the original audio's speed and reduction exactly.)
daily_drills.self_check: Did your shadowed version match the original's actual sound, not a 'corrected', more formal pronunciation?

target_sounds: transcription of a longer, natural conversational exchange

isolation.instructions: Practice transcribing a longer, more natural back-and-forth exchange (2-3 sentences), which combines multiple reduction/linking types at once.
isolation.instructions_ar: تدرب على تفريغ تبادل حواري أطول وأكثر طبيعية (٢-٣ جمل)، اللي بيجمع أكتر من نوع تخفيض/ربط في نفس الوقت.
practice: Listen to and transcribe: 'Didja finish? — Yeah, I shoulda done it sooner, but I got it done.'

pair: accurate full transcription of natural dialogue

sentence_practice: Didja finish the report? Yeah, I shoulda done it sooner.
I gotta go now — it's kinda late already.
Didja hear the news? Kinda surprising, right?
daily_drills.record_this: Didja finish the report? Yeah, I shoulda done it sooner. I gotta go now — it's kinda late already.
daily_drills.self_check: Could you transcribe this natural exchange completely, capturing every reduced form accurately?

target_sounds: listen-transcribe-shadow cycle with unfamiliar native audio

isolation.instructions: Apply the full cycle (listen, transcribe, shadow) to a short clip of unfamiliar native-speed audio (a podcast clip, YouTube video, or similar), per blueprint §5.5's methodology.
isolation.instructions_ar: طبّق الدورة الكاملة (استماع، تفريغ، محاكاة) على مقطع قصير من صوت طبيعي غير مألوف (بودكاست، فيديو يوتيوب، أو مشابه)، بحسب منهجية §٥.٥ من الخطة.
practice: Find a 20-30 second native-speed clip. Listen 3 times. Transcribe it, including reductions. Shadow your transcription while replaying the clip.

pair: full cycle applied to unfamiliar audio

sentence_practice: (No fixed sentences — uses your own chosen audio clip.)
daily_drills.record_this: (Shadow your transcription of a real, unfamiliar native-speed audio clip.)
daily_drills.self_check: How accurate was your transcription compared to the actual audio? Where did you miss reductions or linking?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: transcribing reductions, transcribing linking/flap, shadowing from transcription, natural dialogue, unfamiliar-audio cycle
challenge_sentences: Didja finish? I shoulda called her, but I gotta go pick up an apple and gimme a minute first.
Whaddya want? I kinda need to press the button on the machine, would ya?
I shoulda known — didja hear the news? It's kinda surprising, gotta admit.
daily_drills.record_this: Didja finish? I shoulda called her, but I gotta go pick up an apple and gimme a minute first — whaddya want, anyway?
daily_drills.self_check: Could you transcribe this entire passage accurately by ear, then shadow it matching the natural, reduced pronunciation?

test_yourself.instructions: Complete a full listen-transcribe-shadow cycle on a new 20-30 second native-speed audio clip, then record your final shadowed version.
test_yourself.passage: (Choose a new, unfamiliar native-speed audio clip of your choice — a podcast, interview, or video. Listen, transcribe including all reductions, then shadow it and record your shadowed version.)

sounds_to_check.transcription accuracy: check how many reductions/links were correctly identified
sounds_to_check.shadowing accuracy: check if the shadowed delivery matches the original's speed and reduction

scoring_guide.90_100: Transcription captures nearly all reductions/linking accurately; shadowed delivery closely matches the original's natural speed and sound.
scoring_guide.70_89: Most reductions captured; shadowing is close but occasionally reverts to more formal pronunciation.
scoring_guide.50_69: Transcription captures only the most obvious reductions; shadowing is noticeably slower/more careful than the original.
scoring_guide.below_50: Transcription mostly uses 'correct' formal spelling, missing most reductions — go back and repeat Days 1-4 with simpler, shorter audio clips.

for_arabic_speakers: This week directly implements Blueprint §5.5's Phase 3 (full-speed shadowing) and Phase 4 (transcription-based shadowing) methodology, explicitly assigned to L1-L2 and L2 respectively.

Transcribing casual reductions (shoulda, gonna, whaddya) is a DIFFERENT skill from producing them — many learners can say these forms but struggle to recognize them instantly in fast native speech. This week trains listening comprehension alongside production.
This is excellent preparation for L2's advancement exam Listening section (blueprint Table 45), which tests comprehension of natural, reduced native speech, not slow, careful pronunciation.
supplementary_notes.daily_time: 25-30 minutes this week (includes finding/preparing audio clips for Days 5 and 7)
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 and Day 7's shadowed versions of real audio clips — save the transcriptions too, as evidence of listening comprehension progress.
